                         byNms(2)       warmstart by network management,
                         byWatchdog(3)  warmstart by watchdog,

                         byProcessSuspended(6)  warmstart by process suspended,

                     cardDown(99)    swap by other reason.

             supported.

             objects are supported:
                 zxAnCardMcuVersion 
                 zxAnCardBootwareVersion
                 zxAnCardPn

             zxAnCardLastResetReason is supported

             objects are supported:

             zxAnCardResetTimesClear is supported.

oids.name: zxAnOutOfBandIfTable
oids.description: This table includes the Mng port  infomation.

oids.name: zxAnOutOfBandIfEntry
oids.description: An entry in zxAnOutOfBandIfTable.

oids.name: zxAnOutOfBandIfAdminStatus
oids.description:
The desired state of the interface.  The testing(3) state
            indicates that no operational packets can be passed.  When a
            managed system initializes, all interfaces start with
            ifAdminStatus in the down(2) state.  As a result of either
            explicit management action or per configuration information
            retained by the managed system, ifAdminStatus is then
            changed to either the up(1) or testing(3) states (or remains
            in the down(2) state).

oids.name: zxAnOutOfBandIfLinkStatus
oids.description:
The current operational state of the interface.  The
            testing(3) state indicates that no operational packets can
            be passed.  If ifAdminStatus is down(2) then ifOperStatus
            should be down(2).  If ifAdminStatus is changed to up(1)
            then ifOperStatus should change to up(1) if the interface is
            ready to transmit and receive network traffic; it should
            change to dormant(5) if the interface is waiting for
            external actions (such as a serial line waiting for an
            incoming connection); it should remain in the down(2) state
            if and only if there is a fault that prevents it from going
            to the up(1) state; it should remain in the notPresent(6)
            state if the interface has missing (typically, hardware)
            components.

oids.name: zxAnOutOfBandIfDuplexSpeed
oids.description:
DURABLE: { auto_negotiate:all }
            The desired speed and duplex for this interface. If the selected
            control is not possible on the interface, a value of illegal(99)
            is returned.  If the port type does NOT support the default
            of auto_negotiate(1), then the application initializes the
            port to a valid value (e.g., 1000full(6)).  Note that not all
            controls are possible for all interfaces.  For example, only
            full-1000(6) is available for Gigabit Ethernet interfaces.

oids.name: zxAnOutOfBandIfActualDuplex
oids.description: The actual duplex for this interface.

oids.name: zxAnOutOfBandIfActualSpeed
oids.description: The actual speed for this interface.

oids.name: zxAnOutOfBandIfTagMode
oids.description:
The tag mode for this interface.
            untagged(1):zxAnSysOutbandPortVlanId and zxAnSysOutbandPortCos
            are invalid.
            tagged(2): zxAnSysOutbandPortVlanId is required.
            zxAnSysOutbandPortCos is optional.
            If VLAN ID and CoS are configured, the packet which has the same
            VLAN ID and CoS can be received.
            If only VLAN ID is configured,the packet which has the same VLAN ID
            can be received.

oids.name: zxAnOutOfBandIfVid
oids.description: VLAN ID for this interface. Value 0 means unconfigured.

oids.name: zxAnOutOfBandIfCos
oids.description: CoS value for this interface. Value 255 means unconfigured.

oids.name: zxAnOutOfBandIfConfNegotiation
oids.description:
This object is defined for C600 series NE.
             The desired negotiation for this interface. 
             If the selected control is not possible on the interface, a value 
             of illegal(99) is returned. 
             If this value is auto, zxAnOutOfBandIfConfDuplex and 
             zxAnOutOfBandIfConfSpeed can not be set.
             Only if this value is force, zxAnOutOfBandIfConfDuplex and 
             zxAnOutOfBandIfConfSpeed can be set.

oids.name: zxAnOutOfBandIfConfDuplex
oids.description:
This object is defined for C600 series NE.
             The desired duplex for this interface. 
             If the selected control is not possible on the interface, a value 
             of illegal(99) is returned. 
             If zxAnOutOfBandIfConfNegotiation value is auto, this object can 
             not be set.

oids.name: zxAnOutOfBandIfConfSpeed
oids.description:
This object is defined for C600 series NE.
             The desired speed for this interface. 
             speed10 : 10M,
             speed100 : 100M,
             If the selected control is not possible on the interface, a value 
             of illegal(99) is returned. 
             If zxAnOutOfBandIfConfNegotiation value is auto, this object
             can not be set.

oids.name: zxAnPonOltIfRxCrcAlignErrors
oids.description:
The total number of packets received that
            had a length (excluding framing bits, but
            including FCS octets) of between 64 and 1518
            octets, inclusive, but but had either a bad
            Frame Check Sequence (FCS) with an integral
            number of octets (FCS Error) or a bad FCS with
            a non-integral number of octets (Alignment Error).

oids.name: zxAnPonOltIfRxUndersizePkts
oids.description:
The total number of packets received that were
            less than 64 octets long (excluding framing bits,
            but including FCS octets) and were otherwise well
            formed.

oids.name: zxAnPonOltIfRxOversizePkts
oids.description:
The total number of packets received that were
            longer than 1518 octets (excluding framing bits,
            but including FCS octets) and were otherwise

oids.name: zxAnPonOltIfRxPkts64Octets
oids.description:
The total number of packets (including bad

oids.name: zxAnPonOltIfRxPkts65To127Octets
oids.description:
The total number of packets (including bad
            packets) received that were between 65 and 127 octets 
            in length (excluding framing bits but including FCS octets).

oids.name: zxAnPonOltIfRxPkts128To255Octets
oids.description:
The total number of packets (including bad
            packets) received that were between 128 and 255 octets 
            in length (excluding framing bits but including FCS octets).

oids.name: zxAnPonOltIfRxPkts256To511Octets
oids.description:
The total number of packets (including bad

oids.name: zxAnPonOltIfRxPkts512To1023Oct
oids.description:
The total number of packets (including bad
            packets) received that were between 512 and 1023 octets in length
            (excluding framing bits but including FCS octets).

oids.name: zxAnPonOltIfRxPkts1024To1518Oct
oids.description:
The total number of packets (including bad
             packets) received that were between 1024 and 1518 octets in length
             (excluding framing bits but including FCS octets).

oids.name: zxAnPonOltIfRxBwUtilization
oids.description: Received bandwidth throughput.

oids.name: zxAnPonOltIfRxAvgBwUtilization
oids.description: Received average bandwidth utilization.

oids.name: zxAnPonOltIfRxFragments
oids.description:
The total number of packets received that were less than
         64 octets in length (excluding framing bits but including
         FCS octets) and had either a bad Frame Check Sequence
         (FCS) with an integral number of octets (FCS Error) or a
         bad FCS with a non-integral number of octets (Alignment
         Error).

         Note that it is entirely normal for zxAnPonOltIfRxFragments
          to increment.  This is because it counts both runts (which are
         normal occurrences due to collisions) and noise hits.

oids.name: zxAnPonOltIfRxJabbers
oids.description:
The total number of packets received that were
         longer than 1518 octets (excluding framing bits,
         but including FCS octets), and had either a bad
         Frame Check Sequence (FCS) with an integral number
         of octets (FCS Error) or a bad FCS with a non-integral
         number of octets (Alignment Error).

         Note that this definition of jabber is different
         than the definition in IEEE-802.3 section 8.2.1.5
         (10BASE5) and section 10.3.1.4 (10BASE2).  These
         documents define jabber as the condition where any
         packet exceeds 20 ms.  The allowed range to detect
         jabber is between 20 ms and 150 ms.

oids.name: zxAnPonOltIfTxFragments
oids.description:
The total number of packets sent that were less than
         64 octets in length (excluding framing bits but including
         FCS octets) and had either a bad Frame Check Sequence
         (FCS) with an integral number of octets (FCS Error) or a
         bad FCS with a non-integral number of octets (Alignment
         Error).

         Note that it is entirely normal for zxAnPonOltIfRxFragments
          to increment.  This is because it counts both runts (which are
         normal occurrences due to collisions) and noise hits.

oids.name: zxAnPonOltIfTxJabbers
oids.description:
The total number of packets sent that were
         longer than 1518 octets (excluding framing bits,
         but including FCS octets), and had either a bad
         Frame Check Sequence (FCS) with an integral number
         of octets (FCS Error) or a bad FCS with a non-integral
         number of octets (Alignment Error).

         Note that this definition of jabber is different
         than the definition in IEEE-802.3 section 8.2.1.5
         (10BASE5) and section 10.3.1.4 (10BASE2).  These
         documents define jabber as the condition where any
         packet exceeds 20 ms.  The allowed range to detect
         jabber is between 20 ms and 150 ms.

         packets dropped; it is just the number of times this

oids.name: zxAnPonOltPerfAlmPrfConfTable
oids.description: The performance alarm threshold profile table of interfaces.

oids.name: zxAnPonOltPerfAlmPrfConfEntry
oids.description:
An entry in zxAnPonOltPerfAlmPrfConfTable.
            When a manager wants to create or modify an OLT performance 
            alarm threshold item,it must comply with the multi-variables 
            usage rule that all the mib variables related to same 
            fuction must be in one snmp set operation, the variables have
            only one type: mandatory.
            
            Mandatory variables must be in the one snmp set operation.
                   
            To create an OLT performance alarm threshold item on
            different level, alarm threshold ,restore threshold and 
            zxAnPonOltPerfAlmConfRowStatus should be in one snmp set operation.
            The alarm threshold and restore threshold variables in the 
            one snmp set operationshould be on same level.
            Such as:
            zxAnPonOltPerfRiseAlmThresh and zxAnPonOltPerfRiseAlmClrThresh,
            zxAnPonOltPerfRiseWarnThresh and zxAnPonOltPerfRiseWarnClrThresh,
            zxAnPonOltPerfFallWarnThresh and zxAnPonOltPerfFallWarnClrThresh,
            zxAnPonOltPerfFallAlmThresh and zxAnPonOltPerfFallAlmClrThresh.
            
            To modify an OLT performance alarm threshold item on
            different level,alarm threshold and restore threshold
            should be also in the one snmp set 
            operation regardless of modifying alarm or restore threshold 
            value alone.
            The alarm threshold and restore threshold variables in the 
            one snmp set operationshould be on same level.
            Such as:
            zxAnPonOltPerfRiseAlmThresh and zxAnPonOltPerfRiseAlmClrThresh,
            zxAnPonOltPerfRiseWarnThresh and zxAnPonOltPerfRiseWarnClrThresh,
            zxAnPonOltPerfFallWarnThresh and zxAnPonOltPerfFallWarnClrThresh,
            zxAnPonOltPerfFallAlmThresh and zxAnPonOltPerfFallAlmClrThresh.
            
            To remove an OLT performance alarm threshold item, only 
            zxAnPonOltPerfAlmConfRowStatus should be set to destroy(6) while 
            all levels of this ONU performance alarm threshold item will be 
            removed.

oids.name: zxAnSwManualUpdateRack
oids.description:
The rack number.    
            The multi-variables binding rule should be complied 
            with when a manager manual updates software. According 
            to this rule,all the related mib variables must be 
            included in one SNMP set operation. 
                    
            There are two types of variables: mandatory and 
            optional. All of the mandatory variables must be 
            included in one SNMP set operation. Optional variables 
            may be included in one SNMP set operation with the 
            mandatory variables, but must not be used alone. 
                    
            To manual update software, the mandatory and optional 
            variables are as follows:
            mandatoty: zxAnSwManualUpdateRack, 
                       zxAnSwManualUpdateShelf,
                       zxAnSwManualUpdateSlotList, 
                       zxAnSwManualUpdateSwType
            optional : none
                        
            To manual update subcard software, the mandatory and 
            optional variables are as follows:
            mandatoty: zxAnSwManualUpdateRack, 
                       zxAnSwManualUpdateShelf,
                       zxAnSwManualUpdateSlotList, 
                       zxAnSwManualUpdateSwType,
                       zxAnSwManualUpdateSubcardList
            optional : none

oids.description:
The slot number. updating version file from MP to NP.
            They can be updated in batch. For example: '1,2,3'
            This variable must comply with the multi-variables 
            binding rule described in zxAnSwManualUpdateRack.

oids.name: zxAnSwManualUpdateSwType

The software type is special to update.
            This variable must comply with the multi-variables
            binding rule described in zxAnSwManualUpdateRack.

oids.name: zxAnSwAutoUpdateChkEnable
oids.description:
Enable or disable the software periodic check function.   
                    
                    The multi-variables binding rule should be complied with 
                    when a software periodic check. According to this rule, 
                    all the related mib variables must be included in one SNMP 
                    set operation. 
                    
                    There are two types of variables: mandatory and optional.
                    All of the mandatory variables must be included in one SNMP
                    set operation. Optional variables may be included in one 
                    SNMP set operation with the mandatory variables, but must 
                    not be used alone. 
                    
                    To configure software periodic check(except disable 
                    software periodic check function), the mandatory and 
                    optional variables are as follows:
                    mandatoty: zxAnSwAutoUpdateChkEnable, 
                               zxAnSwAutoUpdateChkStartTime,
                               zxAnSwAutoUpdateChkInterval
                    optional : none

oids.name: zxAnSwAutoUpdateChkStartTime
oids.description:
The start time of software periodic check.            
            For example:yyyy-mm-dd HH:MM:SS.(2001-01-01 02:22:33).   
            
            This variable must comply with the multi-variables binding 
            rule described in zxAnSwAutoUpdateChkEnable.

oids.name: zxAnSwAutoUpdateChkInterval
oids.description:
The interval of software periodic check. 
            Value 0 means unconfigured. 
            Unit is hour.
            
            This variable must comply with the multi-variables binding 
            rule described in zxAnSwAutoUpdateChkEnable.

oids.name: zxAnSwAutoUpdateCurrChkStartTime
oids.description:
The start time of current software periodic check.            
            For example:yyyy-mm-dd HH:MM:SS.(2001-01-01 02:22:33).

The different files name between NE and file server. 
            File name is separated by ',', For example:xxx,xxx,xxx.

oids.name: zxAnSwAutoUpdateChkStatus
oids.description:
The status of software periodic check.
            notStarted(1): Automatic update check not start     
            inProgress(2): Automatic update check in progress
            success(3):    Automatic update check successful
            failed(4):     Automatic update check failed

oids.name: zxAnSwAutoUpdateChkFailedReason
oids.description:
The failed reason of software periodic check.
            noError(1):                       Automatic update check successful
                                              or not start
            fileServerUnconfigured(2):        Automatic update file server
                                              is not configure
            fileServerConnectFailed(3):       File server connect failed
            fileServerLoginFailed(4):         File server configure is not
                                              correct 
            fileServerPathError(5):           File server configure path is 
                                              not correct 
            fileServerProtocolTypeError(6):   File server configure protocol 
                                              type is not correct           
            deviceCheckFailed(7):             NE flash error             
            otherErrors(255):                 Error is not include above list

oids.description:

        notStarted(1):                 Not start
        updateStarting(2):             Update Start
        backingUpFile(3):              Backup software before update
        versionFileAnalyzing(4):       Software analyzing
        versionFileDownloading(5):     Software downloading
        versionFileDownloadComplete(6):Software download completed

oids.name: zxAnSwSwapRack
oids.description:
The Rack No. is used for swap version .
                      The multi-variables binding rule should be complied 
                      with when swap software. According to this rule, 
                      all the related mib variables must be included in one 
                      SNMP set operation. 
                   
                      There are two types of variables: mandatory and optional.
                      All of the mandatory variables must be included in one 
                      SNMP set operation. Optional variables may be included 
                      in one SNMP set operation with the mandatory variables, 
                      but must not be used alone. 
                   
                      To swap software, the mandatory and optional variables 
                      are as follows:
                      mandatoty:zxAnSwSwapRack, zxAnSwSwapShelf, zxAnSwSwapSlot
                      optional :none

oids.name: zxAnSwSwapSlot
oids.description:
The slot number is used for swap version.
                      
                      This variable must comply with the multi-variables
                      binding rule described in zxAnSwSwapRack.

oids.name: zxr10AlmConfirmTimeout
oids.description:

             confirmed.It must be set with zxr10AlmResendingTimes together.

oids.name: zxr10AlmResendingTimes
oids.description:
The resending event times when device does not receive 
            the event confirm message from the snmp manager.
            It must be set with zxr10AlmConfirmTimeout together.

oids.name: zxr10AlmConfirmFlowId
oids.description:
The cleard Flow Id to be confirmed by a snmp manager.
            When a manager receive a trap with confirmation information,
            then the manager should set this mib object to inform the agent
            that this manager has got this event.And then the agent should
            remove this event from a unconfirmed event queue. It must be 
            set with zxr10AlmConfirmAlarmStatus together.

oids.name: zxr10AlmConfirmAlarmStatus
oids.description: The alarm status of the event to be confirmed.

oids.name: zxr10AlmClearBeginFlowID
oids.description:

             end flow ID. 0: the only value you can get.

oids.name: zxr10AlmClearEndFlowID
oids.description:
The clear end flow ID, it must be no less than the begin
             flow ID. 0: the only value you can get.

oids.name: zxr10AlmClearRangeStatus
oids.description:
Alarm clear status. 0: the only value you can get, 
                     1:set the value means to delete the instance.
                     zxr10AlmClearRangeStatus must be set with 
                     zxr10AlmClearBeginFlowID and zxr10AlmClearEndFlowID 
                     together. The clear range number must be less than or 
                     equal to 100. When a manager receive the clear range 
                     oprateion, the current alarms in the range will report 
                     disappear.
